title: mogia_aerothermal_design_1998
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the extracted technical content:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**
    *   NOx = 2.16Rc - 7.175
    *   1.62Rc + 4.211
    *   1.96Rc - 4.688
    *   2.1728Rc - 12.806
    *   2.81Rc - 26.422

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**
    *   **Figure 2 The Landing-Takeoff (LTO) NOx emissions levels of the CFM56 single annular combustors.**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: SLSS Takeoff Pressure Ratio (no units specified)
        *   Y-axis: LTO NOx, g/kN
    *   Curves:
        *   CFM56-2/3: Data points generally increasing with pressure ratio, approximately linear.
        *   CFM56-5BSAC:  Data points generally increasing with pressure ratio, approximately linear.
        *   CFM56-7: Data points generally increasing with pressure ratio, approximately linear.
        *   CFM56-5BPSAC: Data points generally increasing with pressure ratio, approximately linear.
        *   V2500: Data points generally increasing with pressure ratio, approximately linear.
    *   Key Values (estimates from graph):
        * Pressure ratio range: 20 to 36.
        *  NOx range: 30 to 70 g/kn

3.  **Tables:**
    *   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:**
    *   **Figure 3 CFM56-5B Dual-Annular Combustion (DAC) system cross-section.** Diagram of combustion system components, but no labels are specified
    *   **Figure 4 CF6-80C Single-Annular Combustion (SAC) system cross-section.** Diagram of combustion system components, but no labels are specified

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**
    *   Specific fuel consumption (SFC): 9.1 to 11.03 g/kN-s.
    *   LTO NOx slope (CFM SAC combustors): 1.6 to 2.8.
    *   LTO NOx slope (V2500): 1.96
    *   Nominal takeoff pressure ratio: 30
    *   LTO NOx reduction (CFM56-5BP vs CFM56-7B): 9%
    *   Corresponding SFC values: 10.7 and 9.7 g/kN-s
    *   Thrust ratings (CFM56 DAC engines): 100-140 kN
    *   bypass ratio of GE90 engine: more than 8.0
    *   OPR of GE90: exceeding 40 for some models
    *   CF6-45/50 thrust class: 200-230 kN
    *   CF6-80C thrust class: 230-270 kN
    *   E³ lean-combustion DAC technology: for 30 OPR
    *   GE90 OPR = 40 for some of the thrust ratings

title: mogia_aerothermal_design_1998
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the document:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   NOx = 0.1972 P3^0.4 exp(T3/194.4)
*   NOx = 0.0233 T4 P3^0.5 exp(T3/288) / Vref
*   NOx = f [(P3)^0.5 exp(T3/345)]
*   NOXEI = 4.59 x 10^9 F P3^0.25 t exp(0.01t)
*   NOXEI = 1.5 x 10^15 (t - 0.5 Tevap)^0.5 exp(-71100/T)/[P3^0.05 (ΔΡ3/Ρ3)^0.5]

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

Two graphs are shown as "Figure 9: The LM6000 DLE Emissions Signature (ppmv, corrected to 15% O2 dry in exhaust)". The have these similar attributes:
*   **X-axis:** Engine Output, MW
*   **Y-axis (top graph):** NOX, ppmv
*   **Curve 1:** Middle + Inner
*   **Curve 2:** Middle + Outer
*   **Curve 3:** Middle + Inner + Outer
*   **Y-axis (bottom graph):** CO, ppmv

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Operational characteristics, and NOx / CO (ppmv corrected to 15% O2 dry in exhaust) emissions of the LM family of gas turbine engines (standard day, sea level, no inlet/exhaust losses.**

    |                       | LM16000 | LM2500+ | LM6000 |
    | :-------------------- | :------ | :------ | :------ |
    | Power MW              | 15      | 29      | 44      |
    | Thermal Efficiency    | 37%     | 39.1%   | 42%     |
    | Air flow KG/Sec       | 43      | 77      | 125     |
    | Pressure ratio        | 22      | 22      | 30      |
    | Nominal Firing temp °C| 1290    | 1290    | 1290    |
    | NOx/CO Emissions Requirements  |       |     |  |
    | Synchronous idle |          |    35/300   | 40/200   |
    | 25% power |          |    40/50   | 40/50   |
    | 50% power | <50/25         |    <25/20     | <25/20   |
    | 100% power     | <25/20            | <25/20      |  <25/20     |

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   14% (CO increase for E3 technology)
*   LTO NOx of 49.4 (for E3 technology)
*   CO reduction goal of 45%
*   NOx emissions guarantee levels: 25 PPM
*   UHC emissions typically less than 10 PPM

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here is the technical content extracted from the document:

**Table 2: Accuracy goals for next-generation gas turbine combustion models - approximate and CCD-based.**

| Variable                                      | Predictive accuracy band |
| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| NOx                                         | ± 5%                     |
| CO and unburned hydrocarbons                 | ± 10%                    |
| Exhaust SAE Smoke Number                   | ± 2                      |
| SLS idle lean blowout fuel/air ratio         | ± 0.001                  |
| Pattern factor (Peak Profile Factor)          | ± 0.03                   |
| Average radial profile factor                 | ± 0.015                  |
| Maximum liner wall temperature              | ± 25° F                   |
| Combustion system pressure drop             | ± 0.25 (% Pt3)           |
| Diffusion system pressure drop              | ± 0.125 (% Pt3)          |
| Circumferential location of the liner hot spot | ± 0.1 x Nozzle sector    |
| Axial location of the liner hot spot          | ±0.01 x Comb. length    |

        | Chien model        | 0.0390          | 25.918                   | -6.07E-3               |
        | Wilcox             | 0.0390          | 19.225                   | -6.07E-3               |
    *   Method 2: When mass flow rate is matched

        | Turbulence model | Shear velocity ut | Centerline velocity U/ut | Pressure gradient dp/dx |
        |--------------------|-----------------|--------------------------|------------------------|
        | L\_S model         | 0.0442          | 21.16                    | -7.81E-3              |
        | Y\_S model         | 0.0396          | 23.35                    | -6.28E-3               |
        | Chien model        | -               | -                        | -                      |
        | Wilcox             | 0.0485          | 19.4                 | -9.43E-3              |
